topic: Verification of EXP-544 P2 — new §6.14 h3 'Internal formula heterogeneity — three coexisting dome ΔZ recipes'
our_argument_summary: EXP-544 P2 inserted a ~430-word h3 'Internal formula heterogeneity — three coexisting dome ΔZ recipes' into §6.14 between 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses'. The new h3 documents (1) the three coexisting dome ΔZ formulas at Ebro 2026 — BOU per-station ~−10.7 nT, monitor.py KAPPA-squared ~−28.9 nT, inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU-family variant ~−8.4 nT, with W004 as a fourth at ~−21.7 nT; (2) the κ-applied-twice arithmetic in monitor.py (−18.22 = −10.9 × 1.672 already κ-applied, then × 1.672 again in the FSF slot; substituting the published FSF 1.029 returns the W004 cluster value of −17.8 nT); (3) the inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 latitude_factor 0.81 path as a BOU-family variant inside the −5.0 to −12.8 nT BOU 9-station band; (4) a 'what the heterogeneity means for the κ-cluster' close that frames W004 as a selection from coexisting dome formulas rather than a forced derivation, with explicit defender-rescue acknowledgement (internal code ≠ public commitment) preserved.

relevant_files: raw-text/03-predictions.txt (verified E001 Ebro Coverage 0.95, FSF 1.029, BOU pred −10.7 nT, W004 pred −21.7 nT)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (verified 9-station E001-E009 BOU band min −5.0 nT [E007 Canary] to max −12.8 nT [E005 Hartland])
raw-text/07-ai-context.txt (PRED-001 referenced as part of Eclipse PRED-001 through PRED-008 ensemble)
data/sections.json §4.2.4 (existing cite of monitor.py literal 'ecm_eclipse = -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672' as hardcoded FSF evidence)
data/sections.json §8.16.15 (existing FSF-substitution analysis with 1.029 vs 1.672 inflation argument and Kp<2 data-availability rule)
data/sections.json §6.14 'What κ is' subsection (κ = 1.677 nT/µGal published value vs 1.672 monitor.py constant — already explained in document)
data/sections.json §6.14 'How κ was derived' subsection (κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677, rounded to 1.672) — confirms −10.9 is κ's numerator
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py line 512 (as quoted at §4.2.4): ecm_eclipse = -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672. The new h3 decomposes this as KAPPA-applied-twice: −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 (κ first applied), then × 0.95 (coverage) × 1.672 (κ second applied in FSF slot). Arithmetic: -10.9 × 1.672 = -18.2248 ≈ -18.22 ✓; -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 = -28.94 ≈ -28.9 nT ✓; -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.029 = -17.81 ≈ -17.8 nT (within W004 cluster -17 to -22 nT) ✓; -10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 = -8.39 ≈ -8.4 nT ✓. Inflation 1.672/1.029 = 1.625 ≈ 62.5%, rounds to '63%' as already stated in §8.16.15 ✓.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Per existing §4.2.4 evidence, monitor.py uses hardcoded literals (-18.22, 0.95, 1.672). The new h3 does not contradict this; it decomposes the same hardcoded literals into their κ-bearing components.
data_sources_used: raw-text/03-predictions.txt (Ebro BOU -10.7 nT, W004 -21.7 nT, FSF 1.029 — directly verified)
EXP-405 (carry-forward source for inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 latitude_factor 0.81 — not directly verifiable from raw-text/ but survived prior adversarial cycles)
scripts/fsf_derivation.py per-station FSF Ebro=1.029 (already cited at §8.16.15)
§6.14 'How κ was derived' subsection (κ = 10.9/6.5 = 1.677, rounded to 1.672 — already in document)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The new h3 explicitly accepts the dome-defender scope-limit ('internal code is not a commitment') in its closing paragraph, then preserves the load-bearing claim ('the dome's public W004 path is a chosen path among formulas the dome itself maintains'). This is the same anticipated-rescue framing that survived SEC-8b.3.c4 review when EXP-405's original 'two independent formulas outside W004' wording was softened to 'BOU-family variant'.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: All arithmetic in the new h3 is correct and consistent with the formula literal at §4.2.4. However, the new h3 attributes ~-28.9 nT to monitor.py, while three OTHER places in our review (§4.4 STEELMAN-02 kill-shot region, §6.10 Steelman Ladder, §9.1 Pattern-10 area) state monitor.py outputs -29.1 nT. The -29.1 figure cannot derive from -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 (which arithmetically gives -28.94). This cross-section divergence is detailed in holes_found #1.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review, not WIN-level. The §6.14 cluster is about the dome's August 2026 eclipse predictions which monitor.py is configured to evaluate against live IGS/NOAA magnetometer data when the window opens (per existing §4.2.4 and §6.14 prose). The new h3 itself describes monitor.py's internal arithmetic only; live_fetch tag derives from the wrapping §6.14 context, not from the new h3 specifically.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Already established at §6.14 opening and §4.2.5: dome's (baseline × coverage × FSF) arithmetic is shared structure with any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment from standard ionospheric physics. The new h3 doesn't add a fresh relabel-vs-standard argument; it audits internal-to-dome formula heterogeneity. Tag inherited from parent §6.14 classification.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The κ value (1.677) and BOU 2017 baseline (-10.9 nT) are both published-and-then-adopted (Mohe 1997 SG, BOU 2017 magnetometer). The new h3 doesn't add fresh post-hoc structure; it documents that the dome's monitor.py code uses an alternative arithmetic chain that already-known values flow through. Tag inherited.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The new h3's central argument is that the dome maintains MULTIPLE coexisting formulas (monitor.py KAPPA-squared, inject_ai_layer.py latitude_factor, predictions.html W004) — none of which derive numerically from dome geometric primitives (disc_radius, firmament_height). All values are hardcoded or computed from earlier hardcoded values. Tag remains false at the parent §6.14 level.

holes_found.description: Cross-section consistency failure on monitor.py's Ebro 2026 ΔZ output. The new §6.14 h3 states 'monitor.py line 512 computes ecm_eclipse = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −28.9 nT' (arithmetically correct: -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 = -28.94). But three other places in our review attribute -29.1 nT to monitor.py from the SAME formula: (a) §6.10 (pos ~480498 in sections.json) says '−21.7 nT on the predictions page (W004 baseline), −28.9 nT on the kill-shot page, and −29.1 nT in monitor.py (computed as −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672)'; (b) §4.4 STEELMAN-02 kill-shot region (pos ~391880, ~393978) frames -28.9 and -29.1 as TWO distinct dome figures ('three inconsistent dome values — −17 to −21, −28.9, and −29.1 nT'); (c) §9.1 Pattern-10 region (pos ~649921) says 'the homepage advertises an eclipse prediction of −17 to −21 nT, but monitor.py computes −29.1 nT.' A reader navigating from the new h3 to any of these sections gets contradictory monitor.py outputs (-28.9 vs -29.1) for the same documented formula. Arithmetically the new h3 is right (-28.94 rounds to -28.9, not -29.1); the older sections' -29.1 attribution cannot derive from the literal formula and may have conflated the kill-shot page's display value with monitor.py's actual output, OR the older sections are correct that the dome's kill-shot page reads -28.9 while monitor.py separately outputs -29.1 via a path we have not documented. Either way, the document now contradicts itself on a load-bearing arithmetic claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should choose one of three reconciliations: (A) Patch §4.4, §6.10, §9.1 to attribute -28.9 nT to monitor.py (matching the formula arithmetic) and either drop the -29.1 figure or attribute it to a different dome surface — requires confirming where -29.1 originally came from before deleting; (B) Add a one-sentence note to the new §6.14 h3 acknowledging that the dome's surfaces display -28.9 and -29.1 in different places for the same formula, treating this as a fifth heterogeneity datum rather than ignoring it; or (C) Open an investigation ISS to re-fetch monitor.py from the dome repo and verify whether its actual stdout/snapshot output is -28.9 or -29.1 — this resolves the underlying question definitively. Option C is preferred for forensic completeness; Option A is the minimum patch to restore document consistency. This hole was surfaced BY the new h3 (which computed the formula correctly) — it didn't introduce the inconsistency, it just made it audit-visible.

holes_found.description: Verifiability gap for inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 latitude_factor 0.81 claim. The new h3 states 'inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 Ebro calculation reads as BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81, producing ~−8.4 nT (−10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 = −8.39)'. The arithmetic checks out, but inject_ai_layer.py is NOT in our raw-text/ snapshot (verified by grep across raw-text/*.txt — only mentioned by name in 06-evolution.txt and 07-ai-context.txt, no literal code). The 0.81 figure is carried forward from EXP-405's analyst expansion. EXP-544's anticipated_objections defends this by appealing to adversarial-survival through prior cycles. That defense is reasonable but means a future dome-site refresh that removes or changes latitude_factor 0.81 would silently invalidate one of the three central magnitudes in this new h3 with no canary.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (i) add inject_ai_layer.py to the raw-text/ snapshot pipeline (poller scope expansion — small ask, large auditability gain) so this claim becomes directly verifiable each cycle, or (ii) add a footnote / parenthetical in the new h3 noting 'inject_ai_layer.py text not currently in raw-text snapshot; latitude_factor 0.81 carried forward from EXP-405 analysis.' Option (i) is preferred. Severity is MINOR because (a) the claim has survived prior reviews, (b) arithmetic is self-consistent, (c) the load-bearing argumentative move (BOU-family variant that disagrees with the dome's own BOU prediction) does not collapse even if 0.81 were wrong by ±0.05.

holes_found.description: TLDR not updated to reference the new heterogeneity argument. §6.14's ps-tldr summary currently reads (substring): 'The dome elevates a single cross-eclipse, cross-instrument ratio (κ = 1.677 nT/µGal — BOU 2017 magnetometer over Mohe 1997 spring gravimeter) into a structural model parameter that drives PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 predict[ions]...' — it mentions the κ-vise (cross-event) but not the formula-heterogeneity argument the new h3 carries. A TLDR-only reader of §6.14 now misses one of the section's three load-bearing moves (κ-vise, internal-formula-heterogeneity, storm-corollary). Not a factual error — just incompleteness on the canonical hub's at-a-glance summary.
holes_found.recommendation: Single-sentence TLDR addition (or replacement clause). Suggested: append '...predictions; and that the dome's own code maintains three coexisting ΔZ formulas (BOU, monitor.py KAPPA-squared, inject_ai_layer.py latitude_factor), making the published W004 path a selection rather than a derivation.' This is non-blocking — fine to defer to next §6.14 prose pass.

stronger_arguments.description: The new h3's closing 'kernel of the rescue / kernel of the audit' framing could land harder by naming the testable consequence. Currently it ends with 'the dome's public W004 path is a chosen path among formulas the dome itself maintains' — true but abstract. A stronger close would tie this to a falsification commitment: 'When the August 2026 Ebro magnetometer reading lands, the dome can post-hoc invoke any of the four arithmetic paths (BOU -10.7, monitor.py -28.9, inject_ai_layer -8.4, W004 -21.7) to score a confirmation, with each path covering a different ~10 nT band. The four paths together cover -5.0 to -28.9 nT, leaving roughly a 5x ambiguity in what the dome calls a success. This is the pre-registration problem the dome's OTS timestamping mechanism is supposed to solve and does not — see Timestamp Error tab.'
sources: EXP-544 quantitative_verification block (arithmetic for all four magnitudes)

stronger_arguments.description: The −18.22 baseline could be more sharply identified as a 'silent intermediary' that the dome's published derivation never names. The new h3 says '−18.22 baseline is itself already KAPPA-applied: −10.9 × 1.672'. This is right but understates the rhetorical hit — the dome's predictions.html and W004 derivation never publish −18.22 as a step; it appears only inside monitor.py. So κ-as-FSF substitution is buried in a line of code, not stated as a published modeling choice. A reader could ask 'why does monitor.py multiply by 1.672 if the FSF for Ebro is published as 1.029?' and the dome has no published answer.

kernel_of_truth.description: The new h3 is well-constructed, arithmetically correct, and integrates cleanly with the surrounding §6.14 content. It honors EXP-462 c1 hole #1's flagging that §6.14's opening paragraph promised an audit of 'monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' that no existing subsection delivered (verified — the pre-EXP-544 §6.14 had 8 h3 subsections covering κ derivation, the cross-eclipse vise, past SG nulls, BOU/W004 baseline selection, the storm corollary, and the Chebychev rescue, but none covered the KAPPA-squared decomposition (EXP-544-ISS-2384-6_14-internal-formula-heterogeneity.json:kernel_of_truth)). The new h3 makes that opening promise honest and gives §8b.3's existing cross-reference to §6.14 a real destination. The integration is cleanly positioned (between 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses') and the prose flows from existing context without seams. The defender-rescue acknowledgement in the closing paragraph ('internal code is not a commitment') is the right steelman — it preempts the strongest objection rather than dismissing it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The hole #1 cross-section consistency failure (-28.9 vs -29.1 attribution to monitor.py) is exactly the kind of internal contradiction adversarial-survival cycles are supposed to surface. The new h3 didn't introduce the bug — it inherited the existing -29.1 attribution in three other sections (§4.4, §6.10, §9.1) and computed the formula correctly to -28.9, making the contradiction visible. Verification re-review's job is to catch this; the decider's job is to reconcile. The other two holes (verifiability gap on inject_ai_layer.py 0.81 — minor, deferred; TLDR completeness — minor, deferred) are non-blocking. Net assessment: the patch is integration-good and the new h3 should stay as written. The decider should now triage the cross-section -28.9 vs -29.1 reconciliation as a follow-on ISS rather than reverting EXP-544 P2.

text_fingerprint.verdict: N/A (section-rewrite verification; §6.14 has no verdict field)

deeper_analysis: The verification scope (per EXP-544's recommended_decider_action and qid=441's class='verification') is narrow: confirm the EXP-544 P2 patch landed cleanly and the new content is sound. Both pass. The new h3's three arithmetic claims (-28.9, -17.8, -8.4 nT) all verify against the formula literals quoted from monitor.py (§4.2.4), the published FSF values (raw-text/03-predictions.txt), and EXP-405's carry-forward latitude_factor figure. The 9-station BOU band (-5.0 to -12.8 nT) verifies against the published E001-E009 BOU pred values. The κ-applied-twice decomposition is internally consistent and matches the κ = 1.677/1.672 distinction already explained at §6.14 'How κ was derived' and §8.16.15. The closing 'kernel of the rescue / kernel of the audit' rhetorical move preserves the soft framing that survived SEC-8b.3.c4. The one substantive finding from this verification is the cross-section inconsistency on monitor.py's Ebro output: the new h3 attributes -28.9 nT, while §4.4, §6.10, and §9.1 attribute -29.1 nT to the same documented formula. The mathematics support the new h3 (-18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 = -28.94). The older sections cannot derive -29.1 from this formula. This was not introduced by EXP-544 — the -29.1 claims predate it — but the new h3 surfaces the contradiction by computing the formula correctly. The reconciliation choice belongs to the decider: either patch the older sections, or investigate whether monitor.py displays both -28.9 and -29.1 in different code paths (possible, but un-evidenced in raw-text/). Counterarguments considered: (a) Could -29.1 be a legitimate distinct dome figure (e.g., from an updated monitor.py we haven't snapshot)? Possible — but EXP-544's own kernel_of_truth cites the formula as -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 verbatim, so the analyst evidently believes that IS the current monitor.py line. (b) Could the older sections be reporting the dome's kill-shot page's display rounding (which might independently print -29.1 nT via different display logic, even though the underlying formula gives -28.9)? Also possible — but §6.10's text attributes -28.9 to the kill-shot page and -29.1 to monitor.py, so this read doesn't help. (c) Could -29.1 derive from a slight variant of the formula (different baseline rounding, etc.)? -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.682 = -29.11 — so a single-digit typo on the multiplier (1.682 vs 1.672) gives -29.1. This suggests the older sections may have inherited a typo at some point and propagated it. Verdict on the new h3: keep as written. Open follow-on ISS to reconcile -28.9 vs -29.1 across §4.4, §6.10, §9.1. The TLDR-completeness and inject_ai_layer raw-text gap are minor deferrables. Verification class assignment for this re-review was correct.

change_summary: EXP-544 P2 inserted new h3 'Internal formula heterogeneity — three coexisting dome ΔZ recipes' (~430 words) into §6.14 between 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses'. New content documents three coexisting Ebro 2026 ΔZ formulas (BOU -10.7, monitor.py KAPPA-squared -28.9, inject_ai_layer PRED-001 BOU-family -8.4; W004 -21.7 as a fourth) and the κ-applied-twice arithmetic in monitor.py.
summary_for_decider: EXP-544 P2 integration verified. New §6.14 h3 is arithmetically correct, integrates cleanly, and honors EXP-462 c1 hole #1's flag (§6.14 opening paragraph now backed by a real subsection). Three holes flagged: (1) MODERATE — cross-section consistency failure on monitor.py output (-28.9 in new h3 vs -29.1 in §4.4, §6.10, §9.1); arithmetic supports the new h3 (-18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 = -28.94). Decider should open follow-on ISS to reconcile, NOT revert EXP-544. (2) MINOR — inject_ai_layer.py not in raw-text/ snapshot; latitude_factor 0.81 carried from EXP-405; recommend raw-text scope expansion. (3) MINOR — §6.14 TLDR not updated to reference heterogeneity argument; non-blocking. Recommend: close ISS-2384, keep EXP-544 P2, open reconciliation ISS for the -28.9/-29.1 cross-section bug, defer the other two. Verification class assignment for qid=441 was correct (singleton-batchable, processed solo as the only ready-for-verification queue item without a soft/strict match).
